NOTION OF MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON THE GROUNDS OF LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION (CCP 418.10), PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT AMERICAN SAVINGS BANK HEARING SET FOR OCT-27-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT AMERICAN SAVINGS BANK'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON THE GROUNDS OF LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION (CCP 418.10) IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT SHOW PERSONAL JURISDICTION IN CALIFORNIA OVER A HAWAII BANK THAT ISSUED A CHECK IN HAWAII THAT ITS CUSTOMER THEN SENT TO CALIFORNIA. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: RHONDA FUNG CSR # 4819 (301/PJB)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2011 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Tomar Mason, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: B. Fields, CSR #7235

Steven Finley, Esq. appeared for the Plaintiff(s) Raymond Tougas.
Order granting ex parte request to waive live testimony of witness was signed in open court.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) Elias Roussos ind. and dba Pacific Tek for Judgment as follows:

Damages/Principal - $184,000.00
Punitive Damages $600,000.00
Prejudgment Interest - $144,164.38
Costs - $440.00
Total judgment in the amount of $928,604.38

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notion Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Grounds Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction (Ccp 418.10)
Off Calendar Oct-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANT AMERICAN SAVINGS BANK's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On The Grounds Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction (Ccp 418.10) is granted. Plaintiff cannot show personal jurisdiction in California over a Hawaii bank that issued a check in Hawaii that its customer then sent to California. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Rhonda Fung CSR # 4819 (301/PJB)
